reasoning: The index recorded a one-day 9 bps spike to 2.89 on Feb 28, the exact date of month-end rebalancing. Since 2.89 is significantly above the preceding trend (2.80) and technical rebalancing artifacts (like new issue premiums) tend to compress shortly after, there is strong reason to expect an immediate short-term mean-reversion downward. Over the longer term, expert forecasts (e.g., BNP Paribas predicting a 25 bps widening in 2025) note that spreads are at multi-year lows with high dispersion, raising the long-horizon likelihood of a wider spread as the macroeconomic cycle advances. To comply with the instruction that uncertainty increases and probabilities should move closer to 0.5 over longer horizons, the forecast scales smoothly from a highly confident "NO" (likely drop) in the short-term to near 0.5 for the distant dates, reflecting both mean-reversion of the spike and general macro uncertainty.
